code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test30-JUN-2022Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity), suggesting possible renal dysfunction and/or hepatic involvement. Serum glucose and HbA1c are within or below normal, making diabetes unlikely. No evidence of urinary tract infection (negative nitrites, leukocytes). Recommend further evaluation of renal and liver function, and correlation with clinical findings for underlying causes such as glomerular disease or hepatic pathology.
